rationale: The citing paper does more than list this reference: it summarizes Galak et al. (2011) as evidence that crowdfunding lenders favor socially similar borrowers. That empirical point is used in the theory section to support the authors' claim that crowdfunding contexts can activate interdependent self-construal and in-group oriented thinking. This mechanism is central to their hypotheses, so the citation meaningfully informs conceptual framing rather than serving as a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Galak et al.'s Kiva field evidence on similarity-driven prosocial lending as prior empirical support for its mechanism that crowdfunding evokes interdependence. This helps ground the paper's core mediation argument linking crowdfunding transactions to higher demand for social-good products.
